_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-14 Patient Assessment (Revised 5/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-14: Patient Assessment — MDHHS Approved 5/30/2023
purpose: Standardized prehospital patient assessment framework covering scene size-up, primary survey, GCS, AMS differential, and secondary survey.

sections.title: Scene Size-Up
points: Scene safety: identify and mitigate hazards before patient approach
Mechanism of injury or nature of illness
Number of patients — request additional resources if needed
Standard precautions / PPE
Pediatric patients: use Pediatric Assessment Triangle (PAT) — appearance, work of breathing, circulation to skin
Check for DNR or MI-POST form on or near the patient before initiating care

sections.title: Primary Survey — CAB (ABC for Drowning/Respiratory Arrest)
points: C — CIRCULATION / CRITICAL BLEEDING: check pulse; control life-threatening hemorrhage
A — AIRWAY: open and assess airway; suction as needed
B — BREATHING: assess rate, depth, effort; provide supplemental oxygen or ventilatory support as needed
D — DISABILITY: level of consciousness (AVPU or GCS); pupils; gross motor/sensory
Identify and treat life threats during primary survey — do not defer treatment to complete assessment
Drowning and primary respiratory arrest: use ABC sequence (Airway first)

sections.title: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) — Adult (> 2 years)
points: EYE OPENING: Spontaneous = 4; To voice = 3; To pain = 2; None = 1
VERBAL: Oriented = 5; Confused = 4; Inappropriate words = 3; Incomprehensible sounds = 2; None = 1
MOTOR: Obeys commands = 6; Localizes pain = 5; Withdraws from pain = 4; Abnormal flexion = 3; Extension = 2; None = 1
Total GCS range: 3–15
GCS < 8: significant mortality risk; consider advanced airway management

sections.title: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) — Pediatric (< 2 years)
points: EYE OPENING: Spontaneous = 4; To voice = 3; To pain = 2; None = 1
VERBAL: Coos/babbles = 5; Irritable cries = 4; Cries to pain = 3; Moans to pain = 2; None = 1
MOTOR: Normal spontaneous = 6; Withdraws to touch = 5; Withdraws from pain = 4; Abnormal flexion = 3; Extension = 2; None = 1
Total GCS range: 3–15
GCS < 8: significant mortality risk

sections.title: AMS Differential Diagnosis (AEIOU-TIPPS-CHESS)
points: A — Alcohol
E — Epilepsy
I — Insulin (hypoglycemia/hyperglycemia)
O — Overdose
U — Uremia (renal failure)
T — Trauma
I — Ingestion (toxic)
P — Psychiatric
P — Phenothiazines (antipsychotics)
S — Salicylates (aspirin toxicity)
C — Cardiac
H — Hypoxia
E — Environmental (heat/cold)
S — Stroke
S — Sepsis

sections.title: Secondary Survey
points: Vital signs: pulse (rate/quality), respirations (rate/quality), blood pressure, SpO2, temperature (when indicated), blood glucose (when indicated), pain score
Head and face: scalp, skull, face, eyes, ears, nose, mouth
Neck: tracheal position, JVD, crepitus, cervical spine tenderness
Chest: inspection, auscultation, percussion, palpation — bilateral breath sounds
Abdomen: inspection, auscultation, palpation — tenderness, rigidity, guarding
Pelvis: stability, tenderness — compress once; do not repeatedly compress unstable pelvis
Extremities: motor, sensory, circulation, deformity, wounds
Back: posterior thoracic and lumbar spine, flanks
SAMPLE history: Signs/symptoms, Allergies, Medications, Pertinent past history, Last oral intake, Events leading up

deterioration_flags: GCS < 8 — significant mortality risk; manage airway; consider advanced airway if deteriorating
Unequal or non-reactive pupils with AMS — consider head trauma, herniation, stroke; load and go
Hemodynamic instability on primary survey — treat immediately; do not defer for complete assessment
scope_notes: Pediatric patients: use PAT (Pediatric Assessment Triangle) on scene size-up
Check for DNR or MI-POST before initiating care
GCS < 8 is associated with significant mortality — escalate management
